rulings.Case Title: JERRY DUNCAN VS. BRUCE MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 4, 2011, Line 3. DEFENDANTS BRUCE MCCORMACK MD AND EDWARD EYSTER MD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Dr. Eyster¿s motion is off calendar and Dr. McCormack¿s motion is denied. Dr. Eyster was dismissed on September 22, 2011. There is a triable issue whether Dr. McCormack disclosed all pertinent information about the operations and obtained plaintiff¿s informed consent. (See Cobbs v. Grant (1972) 8 Cal.3d 229). Dr. McCormack¿s request for a continuance is denied. A moving party does not have a right to continuance based on purported need to submit additional evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and adding language that complies with CCP 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. G&P CONSTRUCTION INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 4, 2011, Line 1. DEFENDANT FINE BY DESIGN INC.¿S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. Granted. All objections are waived. Verified responses must be served within ten days. Monetary sanctions of $ 450.00 ($205 per hour for two hours plus $40) are awarded against plaintiff and in favor of defendant Fine by Design. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Comp0el Responses To Special Interrogatrories, Set Four And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 4, 2011, Line 2. DEFENDANT DENNIS SHEN'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES SET 4 AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. Denied as moot and sanctions denied. Plaintiff served responses to the discovery that is the subject of the motion. Plaintiff¿s claimed mental health issues and memory loss, while not a legal excuse for failing to respond to the discovery, is a basis not to award sanctions, at least as to this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Javier Milano sufficiently pleads a timely loss of consortium claim. rulings.Case Title: STEVEN FUNK VS. ELIZABETH COLLET FUNK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 4, 2011, Line 16. DEFENDANT ELIZABETH FUNK'S MOTION AND NOTICE OF MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION. Motion to dismiss is granted and the alternate motion to stay is off calendar. The Alberta court is a suitable forum. Judicial economy, the public interest, and the private interests of the parties mandate that all of the parties¿ financial disputes be decided in a single forum. Thus, because of the pendency of the action in Alberta, this court is a seriously inconvenient forum. Moreover, because plaintiff succeeded in his motion in the divorce case based on the argument that ¿California is not the appropriate forum for resolution of the financial issues in this divorce,¿ plaintiff is judicially estopped to oppose this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
